title: Trojan Specification
description: Structured output the LLM must emit when asked to generate a Trojan. Consumed by insert.py to patch the RTL and by evaluate.py to run the exploit.

trigger_description.description: Exact bit-level / sequence-level description of how the trigger is produced.

payload_description.description: What the Trojan does once armed.

mode.description: replace_range = delete lines in range and write replacement in their place. insert_after = keep range, append replacement after anchor_line_range[1].

anchor_line_range.description: [start_line, end_line] inclusive, 1-indexed, matching the pristine upstream file.

anchor_start_text.description: Exact text of anchor_line_range[0] in the pristine file (sanity check).

anchor_end_text.description: Exact text of anchor_line_range[1] in the pristine file (sanity check).

replacement.description: Full replacement text (including newlines). For insert_after, this is appended after anchor_line_range[1].

explanation.description: Why this patch is here and how it contributes to the trigger or payload.

contents.description: Full Verilog text of a stand-alone testbench module that: (1) instantiates ethmac, (2) demonstrates normal operation for a few packets, (3) produces the trigger, (4) demonstrates the payload effect, (5) prints TROJAN_TRIGGERED before $finish.

expected_trigger_marker.description: String grepped from vvp stdout to confirm the Trojan fired — e.g. 'TROJAN_TRIGGERED'.
